document.title: Usability Validation Plan for HAD1717MC / HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation plan UVP-01 Rev.0 for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the usability test plan, participants, scenarios, metrics, acceptance criteria, goals, reporting approach, and referenced usability and risk-management standards.
keyPoints: The plan applies during development of HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
Seven participants are planned for usability testing.
Primary metrics include scenario completion success rates, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
The usability goal is 100% completion rate for each usability task.
The error goal is below 4 total errors for each usability task.

concepts.name: Usability validation plan
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

concepts.name: Critical error
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical error
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Subjective evaluation
concepts.evidence: Subjective opinions about specific tasks, time to perform each task, features, and functionality will be surveyed and collected via questionnaires.

concepts.name: Summative evaluation use scenarios
concepts.evidence: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation

concepts.name: EN 62366-1:2015
concepts.evidence: EN 62366-1:2015 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering to medical Devices

concepts.name: EN ISO 14971:2019
concepts.evidence: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document is Report No. UVP-01 Rev.0.
The document revision history identifies Rev.0 dated Nov 7 2023 as establishment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Nov 7 2023 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The usability test is for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG during development.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG.

evidence.claim: Performance and usage are stated to be the same except for size, with no difference in usability.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'

evidence.claim: A total of seven participants should be used in the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants must meet the user profile covering education, legal qualification, English understanding, and no special experience requirement.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: At least two years of university, equal or more Knowledge: User who meet the legal qualification for each country Language understanding: User who are understand the English Experience: Not require any special experience

evidence.claim: The usability test takes place at the testing lab in a typical operating environment described in the usability engineering report.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will take part in the usability test at the testing lab. The HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG will be used in a typical operating environment described in usability engineering report.

evidence.claim: Participants sign informed consent acknowledging voluntary participation and that participation can cease at any time.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will sign an informed consent that acknowledges: the participation is voluntary, that participation can cease at any time.

evidence.claim: The plan requires individual participant performance not be individually attributable.
evidence.evidenceText: The performance of any test participant must not be individually attributable. Individual participant's name should not be used in reference outside the testing session.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion, dialog script adherence, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: The post-test satisfaction questionnaire uses a 5-point rating scale from 1 strongly disagree to 5 strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: Rating: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating function scenarios include user manual review, cable connections, power on, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions. Familiarize yourself with the user manual... Connecting the power cord... Power on the detector... Arrange the patient in the correct position... Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Hazard-related summative evaluation scenarios cover user manual, preparation, product use, X-ray image implementation, and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation Scenario Task Potential for use errors Check the User manual... Preparation for using the product... Product use... Implementation of X-ray image... Maintenance Cleaning

evidence.claim: Each usability task has a goal of 100% completion rate.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: Each usability task has a goal of below four total errors.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The final usability verification and evaluation report will evaluate metrics against pre-approved goals, subjective evaluations, and usability problems and recommendations.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test. It will consist of a report and/or a presentation of the results; evaluate the usability metrics against the pre-approved goals, subjective evaluations, and specific usability problems and recommendations for resolution.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text.
No issuing company or organization name is present in the extracted text.
Review approver field appears blank while prepared and approval entries are present.
Source text contains quality issues or inconsistencies, including skipped ACT #6 numbering, repeated role text under Test participants, and wording such as 'Are over 100%'.
